event: reddish maculopapular skin rashes on forearms and ankles
grotesque pitting edema (anasarca) involving face, trunk, and extremities
negative family history of autoimmune disorders
no exposure to drugs or toxic substances
diffuse Grade 1/5 Medical Research Council muscular weakness
grotesque pitting swelling of face, neck, abdomen, extremities, and scrotum
multiple erythematous maculopapular rashes on extensor surfaces of elbows and medial malleoli of ankles
normal serologic tests for antinuclear antibodies (ANA)
electromyography showing increased muscle membrane irritability
left vastus lateralis muscle biopsy confirming JDM
reduced C3 and C4 complement levels
diagnosis of systemic capillary leak syndrome (SCLS)
hemodynamic instability managed in neurointensive care unit
amelioration of edema to 50% from baseline
normal motoric power at 2 months post-discharge
taper of prednisolone and azathioprine over 6 months
immunomodulatory therapy tapered off by 1 year
